stories.title: Scaffold NestJS project, install dependencies, remove defaults
stories.goal: Produce a bootable NestJS backend at backend/ with all runtime/dev dependencies installed, the default AppController/AppService removed, and a spec that proves AppModule compiles.

acceptanceCriteria.text: backend/package.json has all required runtime dependencies
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && node -e "const p=require('./package.json'); const r=['@nestjs/core','@nestjs/common','@nestjs/typeorm','typeorm','better-sqlite3','pg','@nestjs/config','class-validator','class-transformer','@nestjs/schedule']; const m=r.filter(d=>!p.dependencies[d]); if(m.length){console.error('missing',m);process.exit(1)}"

acceptanceCriteria.text: default AppController and AppService removed (no files, no registrations)
acceptanceCriteria.check: ! test -f backend/src/app.controller.ts && ! test -f backend/src/app.service.ts && ! test -f backend/src/app.controller.spec.ts && ! test -f backend/src/app.service.spec.ts

stories.notes: Use `npx @nestjs/cli new backend --package-manager pnpm --skip-git --strict` then surgically remove AppController/AppService. The CLI also generates app.controller.spec.ts and app.service.spec.ts — remove those too. The spec file app.module.spec.ts must import AppModule and assert it is a class decorated with @Module. Do NOT add TypeORM, ConfigModule, or any feature module here — those belong to later stories.

stories.title: Configure @shared/* path alias in tsconfig
stories.goal: Wire the @shared/* path alias so backend source can import from the repo-root shared/interfaces/ directory. Prove it with a spec that imports ApiResponse from @shared/interfaces.

regions.reason: compilerOptions.paths — add @shared/* mapping to ../shared/*

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && ! jq -e '.exclude | index("../shared") // false' tsconfig.build.json >/dev/null

acceptanceCriteria.text: backend still builds with alias resolved

stories.notes: Per resolution: do NOT exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json. TypeScript needs to resolve @shared/* types at compile time; they are type-only imports and produce no output in dist/.

stories.title: TypeORM dual-database config (SQLite/Postgres) + AppModule wiring
stories.goal: Create getTypeOrmConfig(configService) that returns Postgres config when DATABASE_URL is set, SQLite config otherwise. Register via TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync + ConfigModule.forRoot in AppModule. SQLite config must mkdirSync('data',{recursive:true}) before returning. Prove all branches with a table-driven spec.

regions.reason: imports block — add ConfigModule and TypeOrmModule imports
@Module decorator imports array — register ConfigModule.forRoot() and TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync({useFactory: getTypeOrmConfig, inject: [ConfigService]})

acceptanceCriteria.text: config factory calls mkdirSync('data', {recursive:true}) for sqlite path
typeorm.config.spec.ts passes (table-driven: DATABASE_URL set → postgres, unset → sqlite)

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q "TypeOrmModule.forRootAsync" src/app.module.ts && grep -q "ConfigModule.forRoot" src/app.module.ts

stories.notes: Per resolution: the glob __dirname + '/../**/*.entity{.ts,.js}' is correct because at runtime typeorm.config.ts compiles to dist/config/, so __dirname = dist/config/, ../ = dist/, glob matches dist/**/*.entity.js. Per resolution: mkdirSync('data', {recursive:true}) MUST be called in the factory before returning the sqlite config — better-sqlite3 won't create the parent dir. The spec must mock ConfigService.get('DATABASE_URL') and assert both branches. Do NOT use synchronize:true in the postgres branch.

stories.title: Create 6 NestJS module scaffolds (agencies, spending, geography, disaster, sync, health)
stories.goal: Scaffold 6 feature modules with the right controller/service shape per module. agencies/spending/geography/disaster get module+controller+service+dto dir. sync gets module+service only (no controller). health gets module+controller only (no service). Register all 6 in AppModule. Prove registration with a spec.

regions.reason: imports block — add imports for the 6 feature modules
@Module imports array — register all 6 feature modules

acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in agencies spending geography disaster sync health; do test -f backend/src/$m/$m.module.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: agencies, spending, geography, disaster each have controller + service
acceptanceCriteria.check: for m in agencies spending geography disaster; do test -f backend/src/$m/$m.controller.ts && test -f backend/src/$m/$m.service.ts || exit 1; done
for m in agencies spending geography disaster; do test -f backend/src/$m/dto/.gitkeep || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: all 6 modules registered in AppModule imports
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q "AgenciesModule" src/app.module.ts && grep -q "SpendingModule" src/app.module.ts && grep -q "GeographyModule" src/app.module.ts && grep -q "DisasterModule" src/app.module.ts && grep -q "SyncModule" src/app.module.ts && grep -q "HealthModule" src/app.module.ts

stories.notes: Per resolution: sync module is module + service only (NO controller) — sync API endpoints belong to Epic 2. Per resolution: health module is module + controller only (NO service). Per resolution: empty dto/ directories must have a .gitkeep so git tracks them. Controllers and services are EMPTY SCAFFOLDS in this story — just the class with @Controller()/@Injectable() decorator and an empty body. No endpoints, no methods. They get implemented in Epic 3 (query API). The modules.spec.ts must assert each module class exists, is decorated with @Module, and (where applicable) registers its controller/provider.

stories.title: Define 5 TypeORM entities with columns, types, relations, and unique constraints
stories.goal: Create the 5 entities per data-model.md with correct column types (all monetary as integer cents, perCapita as integer cents/person, recoveryRatio as float), unique constraints, and register them in their feature modules via TypeOrmModule.forFeature(). Prove with a table-driven entities.spec.ts.

regions.reason: add TypeOrmModule.forFeature([Agency]) to imports
add TypeOrmModule.forFeature([SpendingRecord]) to imports
add TypeOrmModule.forFeature([GeoSpendingSnapshot]) to imports
add TypeOrmModule.forFeature([DisasterFundingRecord, DisasterRecoveryRatio]) to imports

acceptanceCriteria.check: for e in agencies/agency spending/spending-record geography/geo-spending-snapshot disaster/disaster-funding-record disaster/disaster-recovery-ratio; do test -f backend/src/$e.entity.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: monetary columns use integer type (not float)
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -hE '(obligatedAmount|outlayAmount|femaObligated|fedSpendingObligated|perCapita)' src/**/*.entity.ts | grep -v 'number' | grep . && exit 1 || true

acceptanceCriteria.text: SpendingRecord has @Unique on (agencyId, fiscalYear, quarter, awardTypeLabel)
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q "@Unique" src/spending/spending-record.entity.ts && grep -qE "agencyId.*fiscalYear.*quarter.*awardTypeLabel" src/spending/spending-record.entity.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: GeoSpendingSnapshot has @Unique on (stateCode, fiscalYear, agencyId, scope)

stories.notes: Per resolution: perCapita stored as integer cents/person (not dollars). Per resolution: recoveryRatio is dimensionless float — the cents rule does NOT apply. Per resolution: GeoSpendingSnapshot unique constraint includes nullable agencyId; SQLite allows multiple NULL agencyId rows — accept this, dedupe at app level in Epic 2. Use the standard @Unique decorator. Entities use @Column({ type: 'integer' }) for cents fields in sqlite. Use TypeORM metadata in the spec (getMetadataArgsStorage) to assert columns and unique constraints declaratively — do NOT spin up a database for unit tests.

stories.title: Response wrapper interceptor + @Paginated() decorator
stories.goal: Create ResponseWrapperInterceptor that wraps paginated list responses as ApiResponse<T[]> = { data, meta: { total, page, pageSize } } and leaves single-item responses unwrapped. Opt-in via @Paginated() decorator. Import ApiResponse<T> from @shared/interfaces — do NOT duplicate it.

acceptanceCriteria.text: interceptor spec passes (table-driven: list wraps, single item passes through, non-paginated route unaffected)

stories.notes: Per resolution: do NOT create common/api-response.interface.ts. ApiResponse<T> already lives in @shared/interfaces/index.ts — import it from there. The interceptor checks for the @Paginated() metadata via reflect-metadata; if present and the response is an array, wrap as {data, meta:{total:arr.length, page, pageSize}}. If absent or response is not an array, pass through unchanged. The spec must mock ExecutionContext and the next handler, run the interceptor with a table of {input shape, hasPaginated, expected output shape}.

stories.title: CORS, global ValidationPipe, and GET /health endpoint
stories.goal: Enable CORS (FRONTEND_URL env, fallback '*' in dev) and global ValidationPipe (whitelist, transform, forbidNonWhitelisted) in main.ts. Implement HealthController.getHealth() returning { status:'ok', timestamp:ISO, database:'connected' } with a table-driven spec.

regions.reason: top of file — add ValidationPipe and FRONTEND_URL imports
bootstrap() — add app.useGlobalPipes(new ValidationPipe({whitelist:true, transform:true, forbidNonWhitelisted:true})) and app.enableCors({origin: process.env.FRONTEND_URL || '*'})

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q "useGlobalPipes" src/main.ts && grep -q "ValidationPipe" src/main.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: ValidationPipe options include whitelist, transform, forbidNonWhitelisted
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q "whitelist" src/main.ts && grep -q "forbidNonWhitelisted" src/main.ts && grep -q "transform" src/main.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: main.ts enables CORS with FRONTEND_URL fallback to '*'
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q "enableCors" src/main.ts && grep -q "FRONTEND_URL" src/main.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: HealthController has GET /health returning {status, timestamp, database}
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q "@Get" src/health/health.controller.ts && grep -q "status" src/health/health.controller.ts

stories.notes: HealthController injects DataSource (from typeorm) to verify the database connection is active. Return { status:'ok', timestamp: new Date().toISOString(), database: ds.isInitialized ? 'connected' : 'disconnected' }. The spec must mock DataSource and use a table of {isInitialized, expected database field}. ValidationPipe config goes in main.ts bootstrap() — do NOT register it as an APP_PIPE provider in AppModule (keeps the pipe at the HTTP boundary, matching AGENTS.md Skill 1).

stories.title: End-to-end verification of Epic 1
stories.goal: Verify the full Epic 1 surface with one e2e spec that boots the NestJS app, hits /health, and asserts all 5 entity tables exist in the sqlite file. No new production code — only the spec and any small wiring fixes surfaced by the e2e run.

acceptanceCriteria.text: all backend unit specs pass (pnpm test)
no AppController or AppService anywhere in backend/src
e2e spec passes (boots app, hits /health, checks 5 entity tables in sqlite)

stories.notes: The e2e spec uses Test.createTestingModule({ imports: [AppModule] }).compile() then app.getHttpServer() with supertest to GET /health. Then it queries the sqlite file (or DataSource.query) to assert the 5 entity tables exist. If the e2e surfaces a wiring gap (missing module registration, broken import path), fix it inside the files already in scope of prior stories — do NOT create new files. This story's scope is intentionally just the e2e spec; the model may edit previously-committed files only to close wiring gaps surfaced by the e2e.

stories.title: Sync constants, types, and seed agency data
stories.goal: Create the constants module (TRACKED_AGENCIES, AWARD_TYPES, DEF_GROUPS), the request/response TypeScript types for USASpending + OpenFEMA payloads, and a table-driven spec asserting every constant set is well-formed.

acceptanceCriteria.text: sync.constants.ts exports TRACKED_AGENCIES with all 6 agencies
AWARD_TYPES has 5 groups (Contracts, Grants, Direct Payments, Loans, IDVs)
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && for g in 'Contracts' 'Grants' 'Direct Payments' 'Loans' 'IDVs'; do grep -qE "[\"']$g[\"']" src/sync/sync.constants.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: DEF_GROUPS has 5 groups (COVID-19, Hurricane, Infrastructure, Wildfire, General)
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && for g in 'COVID-19' 'Hurricane' 'Infrastructure' 'Wildfire' 'General'; do grep -qE "[\"']$g[\"']" src/sync/sync.constants.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: sync.constants.spec.ts passes (table-driven: agency well-formedness, award type coverage, DEF group coverage)

stories.notes: TRACKED_AGENCIES values per data-model.md: NASA/080, GSA/047, OPM/024, LOC/036, HHS/075, FDIC/581. AWARD_TYPES and DEF_GROUPS per epic-02 spec. Types in usa-spending.types.ts: RawUsaSpendingAwardRow, RawUsaSpendingGeoRow, RawUsaSpendingDisasterRow. Types in openfema.types.ts: RawFemaDeclaration, RawFemaDisaster. All types describe the raw upstream shape; the services in E2-S02/E2-S03 transform them into the entity shapes from Epic 1.

stories.title: UsaSpendingService — Fetch/Transform/Return deep module
stories.goal: Implement UsaSpendingService as ONE deep module that fetches from USASpending.gov, transforms raw rows into cents-integers, and returns typed entity-shaped objects. Methods: fetchAgencies(), fetchSpendingByAgency(agency, fiscalYear), fetchGeoSnapshots(agency, fiscalYear, scope), fetchDisasterSpending(defGroup). Private retry wrapper (3 attempts, exponential backoff). No upsert here — SyncService owns persistence.

regions.reason: providers array — register UsaSpendingService

acceptanceCriteria.text: usa-spending.service.ts exists with all 4 fetch methods
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q 'fetchAgencies' src/sync/usa-spending.service.ts && grep -q 'fetchSpendingByAgency' src/sync/usa-spending.service.ts && grep -q 'fetchGeoSnapshots' src/sync/usa-spending.service.ts && grep -q 'fetchDisasterSpending' src/sync/usa-spending.service.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: service transforms dollar amounts to integer cents
retry logic present (3 attempts, exponential backoff)
usa-spending.service.spec.ts passes (table-driven: transform correctness per award type, transform correctness per geo scope, retry-then-success, retry-then-failure)

stories.notes: Deep module per AGENTS.md Skill 3: ONE file, top-to-bottom legible. The Fetch (HTTP), Transform (raw dollars → cents), and Return (typed) steps stay together. Use global fetch() — no axios dependency. Mock fetch in the spec via jest.spyOn(global, 'fetch'). The spec testTable must cover: (a) raw dollar field 1234.56 → obligatedAmount 123456 cents, (b) pagination handled across multiple pages, (c) HTTP 500 then 200 → retry succeeds, (d) HTTP 500 thrice → throws after 3 attempts. Do NOT call the real USASpending API in unit tests — all fetches mocked.

stories.title: OpenFemaService — Fetch/Transform/Return deep module
stories.goal: Implement OpenFemaService that fetches disaster declarations from OpenFEMA, aggregates them by state and fiscal year, and returns typed objects: { stateCode, stateName, fiscalYear, femaObligatedCents, declarationCount, dominantIncidentType }. Same retry pattern as UsaSpendingService.

regions.reason: providers array — register OpenFemaService

acceptanceCriteria.text: service groups declarations by stateCode + fiscalYear
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -qE '(groupBy|reduce).*stateCode' src/sync/openfema.service.ts || grep -qE 'for.*of.*declarations' src/sync/openfema.service.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: dominant incident type computed per state (most frequent)
openfema.service.spec.ts passes (table-driven: aggregation, dominant incident, retry)

stories.notes: Same deep-module shape as UsaSpendingService. OpenFEMA endpoint: GET https://www.fema.gov/api/open/v2/DisasterDeclarationsSummaries. Declarations have incidentType, state, declarationDate, obligatedAmount (dollars). Group by state + fiscalYear (derive from declarationDate). Dominant incidentType = mode across all declarations in that state-year. femaObligated converted to cents. Mock fetch in spec; do not hit real API.

stories.title: Recovery ratio calculator — pure module with edge cases
stories.goal: Implement a pure function computeRecoveryRatio(femaCents, fedCents) that returns DisasterRecoveryRatio shape. Handles 4 cases: both zero → 1.0, fema zero fed positive → Infinity, fema positive fed zero → 0, both positive → fed/fema. Table-driven spec covers all 4 + boundary values.

acceptanceCriteria.text: recovery-ratio.spec.ts passes (table-driven across 4 edge cases + boundaries)
function is pure (no service injection, no side effects)

stories.notes: Edge cases per epic-02 spec: both 0 → 1.0 (neutral); fema 0 fed >0 → Infinity (well-resourced); fema >0 fed 0 → 0 (complete gap); both >0 → fed/fema. Spec testTable must include boundary values like {fema:1, fed:Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER} to verify no overflow. This is a pure helper — not @Injectable — because it has no dependencies. SyncService in E2-S05 imports and calls it.

stories.title: SyncService — orchestration, upserts, retry coordination
stories.goal: Implement SyncService as the orchestrator: calls UsaSpendingService + OpenFemaService, transforms returned shapes into entities, upserts via TypeORM repositories, computes recovery ratios via computeRecoveryRatio, and exposes syncAll(), syncAgenciesAndSpending(), syncGeography(), syncDisaster() methods. Track per-module timestamps in an in-memory map (persisted via SyncStatusEntity is OUT of scope — Epic 2 uses in-memory only, surfaced by GET /sync/status).

regions.reason: providers + imports — register SyncService, import feature modules for repository access (AgenciesModule etc. need exports)

acceptanceCriteria.text: sync.service.ts exposes syncAll, syncAgenciesAndSpending, syncGeography, syncDisaster
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q 'syncAll' src/sync/sync.service.ts && grep -q 'syncAgenciesAndSpending' src/sync/sync.service.ts && grep -q 'syncGeography' src/sync/sync.service.ts && grep -q 'syncDisaster' src/sync/sync.service.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: SyncService uses upsert pattern (insert-or-update) for all 5 entity types

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q 'computeRecoveryRatio' src/sync/sync.service.ts && grep -q 'recovery-ratio' src/sync/sync.service.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: sync.service.spec.ts passes (table-driven: agencies upsert path, spending upsert path, geo upsert path, disaster upsert path, recovery ratio stored correctly)

stories.notes: SyncService is the deep module that ties Epic 2 together. Inject UsaSpendingService, OpenFemaService, and 5 repositories (Agency, SpendingRecord, GeoSpendingSnapshot, DisasterFundingRecord, DisasterRecoveryRatio). Upsert = findOne by unique key + save (or repository.upsert if TypeORM supports the unique constraint natively). Mock all 5 repositories AND both upstream services in the spec — the spec must NOT hit any real DB or HTTP. The testTable should cover each of the 4 sync methods individually plus the orchestration of syncAll.

stories.title: SyncController — POST /sync/* endpoints
stories.goal: Add SyncController with 5 endpoints: POST /sync, POST /sync/agencies, POST /sync/geography, POST /sync/disaster, GET /sync/status. Thin controllers per AGENTS.md: parse (no DTO for POST triggers, just an empty body), call one SyncService method, return result. Register in SyncModule.

regions.reason: controllers array — register SyncController

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && for m in syncAll syncAgenciesAndSpending syncGeography syncDisaster getStatus; do grep -q "$m" src/sync/sync.controller.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: sync.controller.spec.ts passes (table-driven: each endpoint calls the right service method, returns expected wrapper shape)

stories.notes: Thin controller: each method is 2-3 lines — parse DTO (empty for triggers), call one service method, return result. Use @Sse() or just @Post() per endpoint. GET /sync/status returns the in-memory timestamp map from SyncService. Spec mocks SyncService and asserts each endpoint calls the expected method with no args (for full sync) and returns the result. Use @Controller('sync') prefix.

stories.title: Nightly schedule via @nestjs/schedule (2am UTC cron)
stories.goal: Wire ScheduleModule.forRoot() into AppModule and add a @Cron('0 2 * * *') decorator on SyncService.syncAll(). Verify the schedule registers via logs + a unit test that the cron decorator is present on the method.

regions.reason: imports block — add ScheduleModule.forRoot() import
@Module imports array — register ScheduleModule.forRoot()
imports — add @Cron decorator import from @nestjs/schedule

stories.notes: Do NOT actually fire the cron in unit tests — that requires real time. The spec asserts presence of the @Cron decorator on syncAll via reflect-metadata, and that ScheduleModule is in AppModule imports. To verify the cron fires at the right time, you'd need an integration test that's out of scope for Epic 2. Just register it correctly.

stories.title: End-to-end verification of Epic 2 (mocked HTTP)
stories.goal: E2E spec that boots the app with all sync modules wired, mocks the upstream HTTP APIs (USASpending + OpenFEMA), triggers POST /sync, and asserts rows appear in all 5 entity tables with correct cents values. No real network calls.

acceptanceCriteria.text: sync e2e spec passes (POST /sync with mocked upstreams → rows in 5 tables)

stories.notes: Use Test.createTestingModule({imports:[AppModule]}).overrideProvider(UsaSpendingService).useValue(mockUsa) etc. Then supertest POST /sync and inspect the (in-memory or sqlite) DB. Assert: 6 Agency rows, at least 1 SpendingRecord per FY per agency, GeoSpendingSnapshots for sampled states, DisasterFundingRecords per DEF group, DisasterRecoveryRatios with computed values. Verify NO real HTTP was issued by checking the mocks received the expected calls. This spec's scope is just the e2e file; it may edit prior files only to close wiring gaps surfaced by the run.

stories.title: Query DTOs with class-validator + global ValidationPipe wiring
stories.goal: Create DTOs for every query endpoint (PaginationDto, GeographyQueryDto, AgencySpotlightQueryDto, DisasterStatesQueryDto, DisasterRatiosQueryDto) with class-validator decorators. Fiscal year 2020-2024, page default 1, pageSize default 15 max 100, scope enum, defGroup enum. ValidationPipe already exists from E1-S07 — this story only adds DTOs.

acceptanceCriteria.check: for f in backend/src/agencies/dto/agency-spotlight-query.dto.ts backend/src/geography/dto/geography-query.dto.ts backend/src/disaster/dto/disaster-states-query.dto.ts backend/src/disaster/dto/disaster-ratios-query.dto.ts backend/src/common/pagination.dto.ts; do test -f $f || exit 1; done

cd backend && for f in src/agencies/dto/agency-spotlight-query.dto.ts src/geography/dto/geography-query.dto.ts src/disaster/dto/disaster-states-query.dto.ts src/disaster/dto/disaster-ratios-query.dto.ts src/common/pagination.dto.ts; do grep -qE '@Is(Int|Optional|Enum|String)' "$f" || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: dtos.spec.ts passes (table-driven: each DTO accepts valid input, rejects invalid)

stories.notes: Use class-validator + class-transformer. Validation happens via the global ValidationPipe from E1-S07 (whitelist+transform+forbidNonWhitelisted). Test each DTO by instantiating it and running validate() from class-validator; testTable covers: valid fiscalYear=2024, invalid fiscalYear=2019, missing scope, pageSize over 100, etc.

stories.title: Agencies query endpoints (list, summary, spotlight)
stories.goal: Implement AgenciesService.query methods and AgenciesController endpoints: GET /agencies, GET /agencies/:id/summary, GET /agencies/:id/spotlight. List returns 6 agencies with current FY totals; summary returns current+prior FY totals + YoY change; spotlight returns SpendingRecord[] grouped by fiscalYear + awardTypeLabel.

regions.reason: exports — add AgenciesService to module exports so SyncModule can use it if needed; ensure controllers array has AgenciesController

acceptanceCriteria.text: AgenciesService has findAllWithTotals, findSummary, findSpotlight methods
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q 'findAllWithTotals' src/agencies/agencies.service.ts && grep -q 'findSummary' src/agencies/agencies.service.ts && grep -q 'findSpotlight' src/agencies/agencies.service.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: agencies.service.spec.ts passes (table-driven: totals aggregation, YoY calc, spotlight grouping)
agencies.controller.spec.ts passes (table-driven: each endpoint calls service with correct args, returns ApiResponse<T> for list endpoints)

stories.notes: List endpoint wraps in ApiResponse<T[]> (paginated). Single-item endpoints return T directly (no wrapper) per E1-S06. YoY change = (current - prior) / prior * 100, guard divide-by-zero (return 0 if prior=0). Spotlight groups by fiscalYear then awardTypeLabel.

stories.title: Geography query endpoints (states list, state detail)
stories.goal: Implement GeographyService + endpoints: GET /geography/states?fiscalYear=&agencyId=&scope= returns GeoSpendingSnapshot[] sorted by obligatedAmount desc; GET /geography/state/:code returns all years+agencies for one state.

regions.reason: exports + controllers — register GeographyService in exports and GeographyController in controllers

acceptanceCriteria.text: GeographyService has queryStates and getStateDetail methods
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q 'queryStates' src/geography/geography.service.ts && grep -q 'getStateDetail' src/geography/geography.service.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: nullable agencyId handled (returns combined-agency data when null/missing)
geography.service.spec.ts passes (table-driven: query by FY, filter by agency, filter by scope, sort desc, state detail returns all years)

stories.notes: Mock repositories in spec. testTable must cover: (a) filter by fiscalYear only, (b) filter by fiscalYear + agencyId, (c) filter by fiscalYear + scope=recipient, (d) nullable agencyId returns IsNull() query, (e) sorted by obligatedAmount desc.

stories.title: Disaster query endpoints (overview, states, ratios, state detail)
stories.goal: Implement DisasterService + 4 endpoints: GET /disaster/overview (one row per DEF group with totals), GET /disaster/states?defGroup=&fiscalYear= (DisasterFundingRecord[] sorted by obligatedAmount desc), GET /disaster/recovery-ratios?fiscalYear= (DisasterRecoveryRatio[] sorted by ratio asc), GET /disaster/state/:code (full profile).

regions.reason: exports + controllers — register DisasterService and DisasterController

acceptanceCriteria.text: DisasterService has getOverview, queryStates, queryRatios, getStateProfile methods
acceptanceCriteria.check: cd backend && grep -q 'getOverview' src/disaster/disaster.service.ts && grep -q 'queryStates' src/disaster/disaster.service.ts && grep -q 'queryRatios' src/disaster/disaster.service.ts && grep -q 'getStateProfile' src/disaster/disaster.service.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: disaster.service.spec.ts passes (table-driven: overview aggregation, states by DEF group, ratios sort, state profile shape)

stories.notes: Overview aggregates per DEF group: totalObligated, totalAwardCount, stateCount, highestPerCapitaState, coverageGapCount (states with ratio<0.5). Ratios sorted ASC = worst-covered first (ratio near 0 = gap). State profile combines all DEF groups for one state + its ratio + declaration count.

stories.title: E2E query API tests with seeded data
stories.goal: E2E spec that boots the app with seeded test data (6 agencies + sample spending/geo/disaster rows) and asserts every query endpoint returns the correct shape + filtered results.

acceptanceCriteria.text: query e2e spec passes (every endpoint exercised with seed data)

stories.notes: seed.ts inserts 6 agencies + 5 sample SpendingRecords + 5 GeoSpendingSnapshots + 5 DisasterFundingRecords + 5 DisasterRecoveryRatios into an in-memory sqlite. E2E spec uses Test.createTestingModule + supertest to hit every endpoint and assert response shape + filtering correctness. Do NOT test validation rejection here (covered in DTO specs).

stories.title: Scaffold Angular 20 app + install Material, ng2-charts, jest-preset-angular
stories.goal: Run `npx --yes @angular/cli@20 new frontend --style=scss --ssr=false --skip-git --skip-install --package-manager=pnpm --defaults`. Add 'frontend' to pnpm-workspace.yaml, then `pnpm install` at repo root. Install @angular/material, @angular/cdk, ng2-charts, chart.js. Swap Karma for jest-preset-angular (jest@^30, jsdom@^26). Add @shared/* path alias. Verify with a sanity spec.

acceptanceCriteria.check: test -f frontend/jest.config.js && test -f frontend/setup-jest.ts && ! test -f frontend/karma.conf.js

stories.notes: Use the Angular 20 standalone API (default — no NgModules). ng new flags: --skip-git (avoid a nested repo inside the build worktree), --skip-install (this is a pnpm workspace; register 'frontend' in pnpm-workspace.yaml then run `pnpm install` at the REPO ROOT so the single root pnpm-lock.yaml covers both backend and frontend). Add runtime deps to the frontend workspace package with `pnpm add --filter frontend @angular/material @angular/cdk ng2-charts chart.js`, and devDeps with `pnpm add -D --filter frontend jest-preset-angular jest @types/jest jsdom` (jest-preset-angular@latest requires Angular >=20, jest ^30, jsdom ^26). Remove karma.conf.js and the karma-related devDeps (karma, karma-chrome-launcher, karma-coverage, karma-jasmine, jasmine-core) and remove the 'test' builder entry in angular.json; set the frontend package.json 'test' script to jest. jest.config.js: preset 'jest-preset-angular', setupFilesAfterEnv ['<rootDir>/setup-jest.ts'], testEnvironment 'jsdom'. setup-jest.ts: `import 'jest-preset-angular/setup-jest';`. tsconfig.spec.json compilerOptions.types: ['jest','node']. Add to frontend/tsconfig.json compilerOptions.paths: "@shared/*": ["../shared/*"] (no baseUrl needed under TS5). app.spec.ts asserts the root AppComponent compiles. The whole frontend/ tree is committed via scope.commitAllUnder (you create it with the CLI); the root pnpm-lock.yaml is also auto-staged.

stories.title: 5 routes with placeholder standalone components
stories.goal: Create 5 standalone placeholder components (DashboardComponent, GeographicViewComponent, AgencyListComponent, AgencySpotlightComponent, DisasterLensComponent) and define the 5 routes in frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts. The Angular 20 scaffold already wires provideRouter via app.config.ts — do NOT add a second router provider and do NOT edit main.ts. Verify all 5 route->component mappings via a table-driven routing spec.

acceptanceCriteria.check: for c in dashboard/dashboard geographic-view/geographic-view agency-list/agency-list agency-spotlight/agency-spotlight disaster-lens/disaster-lens; do test -f frontend/src/app/$c.component.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: router is provided via app.config.ts (scaffold wiring intact)
routes.spec.ts passes (table-driven: each path navigates to the right component)

stories.notes:
Angular 20 standalone components. CRITICAL — the scaffold ALREADY wires routing: app.config.ts has providers: [provideBrowserGlobalErrorListeners(), provideZoneChangeDetection({eventCoalescing:true}), provideRouter(routes)] importing `routes` from './app.routes', and main.ts does bootstrapApplication(App, appConfig). So: DO NOT create a separate routes.ts; DO NOT add a second provideRouter anywhere (double-provisioning breaks the router at runtime); DO NOT edit main.ts or app.config.ts. Your ONLY routing change is populating frontend/src/app/app.routes.ts (replace `export const routes: Routes = [];`). Tasks: (1) Create 5 standalone placeholder components, each in .component.ts exporting class Component — DashboardComponent (dashboard), GeographicViewComponent (geographic-view), AgencyListComponent (agency-list), AgencySpotlightComponent (agency-spotlight), DisasterLensComponent (disaster-lens). Components are standalone BY DEFAULT in Angular 20 (omit the `standalone` flag). Each @Component: selector 'app-', templateUrl '.component.html', and the .component.html contains a single

heading

. No imports needed for placeholders. (2) In app.routes.ts define: { path: '', component: DashboardComponent }, { path: 'geography', component: GeographicViewComponent }, { path: 'agencies', component: AgencyListComponent }, { path: 'agencies/:id', component: AgencySpotlightComponent }, { path: 'disaster', component: DisasterLensComponent } — import each component, use single-quoted path strings, eager `component:` mapping. (3) routes.spec.ts: TABLE-DRIVEN — import `routes` from './app.routes' and all 5 component classes; build a testTable of {path, expectedComponent} rows; for each row, find the route in `routes` with that path and assert route.component === expectedComponent. No RouterTestingModule/harness needed — assert directly on the routes array. The root component class is `App` (Angular 20 renamed it from AppComponent), referenced only by main.ts — leave it alone.

stories.title: ApiService with typed methods for every backend endpoint
stories.goal: Implement ApiService as the single deep module owning all HTTP. Methods per epic-04 spec: getAgencies, getAgencySpotlight, getAgencySummary, getGeographyStates, getGeographyState, getDisasterOverview, getDisasterStates, getDisasterRecoveryRatios, getDisasterState. All return Observable<T> typed via @shared/interfaces. catchError returns safe fallbacks (empty arrays).

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd frontend && for m in getAgencies getAgencySpotlight getAgencySummary getGeographyStates getGeographyState getDisasterOverview getDisasterStates getDisasterRecoveryRatios getDisasterState; do grep -q "$m(" src/app/api.service.ts || exit 1; done

acceptanceCriteria.text: all methods return Observable<T> typed via @shared/interfaces

acceptanceCriteria.check: cd frontend && grep -q 'apiUrl' src/environments/environment.ts && test -f src/environments/environment.prod.ts

acceptanceCriteria.text: api.service.spec.ts passes (table-driven: each method calls HttpClient with correct URL + params, returns parsed data)

stories.notes: Single deep module per AGENTS.md Skill 3 — one ApiService, one method per use case, HttpClient injected, base URL from environment.apiUrl. provideHttpClient is NOT wired in this story (deferred to E4-S04); tests use provideHttpClientTesting + HttpTestingController, so do NOT edit app.config.ts. CRITICAL RESPONSE SHAPES — the backend has NO @Paginated decorator, so its ResponseWrapperInterceptor passes everything through RAW. ONLY GET /agencies returns a wrapped {data,meta} object (the service pre-wraps it); every other endpoint returns a raw array or raw object. So ONLY getAgencies maps r.data; the other 8 methods return the body directly (r). Per-method contract: getAgencies() GET /agencies -> {data,meta}; map r.data; the item shape is {id,name,totalCents} (NOT the shared Agency shape — backend drift), so define a local AgencyWithTotal interface. getAgencySummary(id) GET /agencies/:id/summary -> raw AgencySummary | null; return r. getAgencySpotlight(id) GET /agencies/:id/spotlight -> raw SpendingRecord[] | null; return r. getGeographyStates(params) GET /geography/states with HttpParams {fiscalYear?,agencyId?,scope?} -> raw GeoSpendingSnapshot[]; return r. getGeographyState(code) GET /geography/state/:code -> raw GeoSpendingSnapshot[]; return r. getDisasterOverview() GET /disaster/overview -> raw ARRAY DisasterOverview[] (note: array, not a single object); return r. getDisasterStates(params) GET /disaster/states with HttpParams {defGroup?,fiscalYear?} -> raw DisasterFundingRecord[]; return r. getDisasterRecoveryRatios(params) GET /disaster/recovery-ratios with HttpParams {fiscalYear?} -> raw DisasterRecoveryRatio[]; return r. getDisasterState(code) GET /disaster/state/:code -> raw state-profile object (no matching @shared/interfaces type — define a local DisasterStateProfile interface). Import matching types from @shared/interfaces (GeoSpendingSnapshot, DisasterRecoveryRatio, SpendingRecord, AgencySummary, DisasterOverview, ApiResponse). Build HttpParams skipping undefined values. catchError fallbacks: of([] as T[]) for list methods, of(null) for single/null methods. Spec (api.service.spec.ts) TABLE-DRIVEN: one testTable row per method {name, methodName, args, expectedUrl, mockBody, expected}; TestBed with provideHttpClientTesting, inject HttpTestingController; for each row call the method, expectOne(expectedUrl), flush mockBody (the REAL backend shape per the contract above), assert the emitted value equals expected (post-map: r.data for getAgencies, the body for the rest). No comments in code.

stories.title: LoadingService + LoadingInterceptor + ErrorInterceptor
stories.goal: LoadingService exposes `loading$: WritableSignal<boolean>`. LoadingInterceptor increments/decrements a counter on every HTTP request, sets loading$ true when >0. ErrorInterceptor catches non-2xx, shows mat-snack-bar with the error message, does NOT retry (re-throws so downstream catchError fallbacks still fire). Wire both via provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([loadingInterceptor, errorInterceptor])) in app.config.ts, and add provideAnimationsAsync() so MatSnackBar works at runtime.

acceptanceCriteria.text: app.config.ts registers both interceptors via withInterceptors([...])
app.config.ts provides animations (MatSnackBar requires it at runtime)

stories.notes: Angular 20. CRITICAL: providers go in app.config.ts, NOT main.ts. main.ts just does bootstrapApplication(App, appConfig) — do NOT edit it. In app.config.ts add to the EXISTING providers array: provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([loadingInterceptor, errorInterceptor])) AND provideAnimationsAsync() (import from @angular/platform-browser/animations/async — MatSnackBar needs animations at runtime; without it, opening a snack-bar throws). Keep the existing provideRouter(routes), provideBrowserGlobalErrorListeners(), and provideZoneChangeDetection. Interceptors are FUNCTIONAL (HttpInterceptorFn from @angular/common/http), NOT classes. LoadingService (@Injectable({providedIn:'root'})): a private counter field, increment()/decrement() methods, and readonly loading$ = signal(false) as a WritableSignal<boolean> (signal from @angular/core). decrement clamps counter at >=0; both methods call loading$.set(this.counter > 0). Only loading$ and increment/decrement are public. LoadingInterceptor (HttpInterceptorFn): inject(LoadingService), call increment() before forwarding, return next(req).pipe(finalize(() => loadingSvc.decrement())) so the counter decrements on BOTH success and error. ErrorInterceptor (HttpInterceptorFn): inject(MatSnackBar), return next(req).pipe(catchError((error: HttpErrorResponse) => { const msg = error.status ? error.status + ': ' + error.message : 'Network error'; snackBar.open(msg, 'Dismiss', { duration: 5000 }); return throwError(() => error); })) — RE-THROW after showing the snack-bar so the ApiService catchError fallbacks still fire (the service still returns [] / null to its subscribers); do NOT swallow. Specs: loading.service.spec.ts TABLE-DRIVEN over a testTable of {operations, expectedLoading} rows asserting loading$() after increment/decrement sequences. interceptors.spec.ts: TestBed with provideHttpClient(withInterceptors([loadingInterceptor, errorInterceptor])) + provideHttpClientTesting, HttpTestingController; for LoadingInterceptor assert the LoadingService counter/loading$ toggles around a flushed request; for ErrorInterceptor spy on MatSnackBar.open (inject MatSnackBar, jest.spyOn) and assert open() is called with the message AND the error re-throws (subscribe with error handler). No comments in code.

stories.title: Geographic View — recipient vs performance delta insight
stories.goal: When scope is toggled, compute and show a delta stat for the top state: 'California contractors receive $X but only $Y worth of work happens in-state.' Requires loading both recipient and performance scope data for the comparison.

acceptanceCriteria.text: component fetches both scopes for delta computation
delta computed for top state by obligatedAmount
geographic-view.component.spec.ts passes (delta stat for testTable of recipient+performance data)

stories.notes:
When user selects scope=recipient, ALSO fetch performance data (and vice versa) to compute the delta. Show delta stat only when both scopes loaded successfully. Delta = recipient$ - performance$ for the top state.

MUST FIX (from E5-S02 QA findings — .research/qa-findings/E5-S02.md):
1. Delta-sign bug: E5-S02 computes delta = selectedScope - oppositeScope, which flips sign when scope=performance. Delta must ALWAYS be recipient - performance regardless of which scope is selected. The existing test row 2 (named "negative") asserts +1_000_000_000 — this assertion is WRONG and must be corrected to assert the negative value.
2. agencyList getter bug: E5-S02 returns [] once an agency is selected (agencyId() === null ? [...] : []). The dropdown empties on selection. Fix: always return the full agency list.

resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows sync/sync.controller.ts but Step 8 CLI command uses --no-controller for sync module
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT create sync/sync.controller.ts in Epic 1. Only create sync.module.ts and sync.service.ts.
resolutions.reason: CLI command is the executable spec. Sync API endpoints belong to Epic 2. The sync module in Epic 1 is infrastructure only.

resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows common/api-response.interface.ts but ApiResponse<T> already exists in shared/interfaces/index.ts
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT create common/api-response.interface.ts. Import ApiResponse<T> from @shared/interfaces in the interceptor.
resolutions.reason: Duplicating the interface creates drift risk. shared/interfaces/ exists for cross-layer type sharing.

resolutions.contradiction: Epic Step 5 says to possibly exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json
resolutions.resolution: Do NOT exclude ../shared from tsconfig.build.json. TypeScript needs to resolve @shared/* types for compilation. These are type-only imports and do not produce output in dist/.
resolutions.reason: Excluding ../shared would prevent tsc from resolving @shared/* imports at compile time, causing build failures.

resolutions.contradiction: Entity glob pattern __dirname + '/../**/*.entity{.ts,.js}' appears to go up from config/ — is this correct?
resolutions.resolution: The glob is correct as written. At runtime, typeorm.config.ts is compiled to dist/config/typeorm.config.js, so __dirname is dist/config/. Going ../ reaches dist/. The glob dist/**/*.entity.js matches all compiled entities.
resolutions.reason: Traced compilation path: src/config/ compiles to dist/config/, __dirname = dist/config/, ../ = dist/, glob matches dist/agencies/agency.entity.js etc.

resolutions.contradiction: data/ directory does not exist and is not tracked by git; better-sqlite3 will fail if parent directory is missing
resolutions.resolution: The TypeORM config factory must call mkdirSync('data', { recursive: true }) before returning the SQLite config object.
resolutions.reason: better-sqlite3 does not create parent directories. The config factory is the correct place to ensure the directory exists before the database file is created.

resolutions.contradiction: GeoSpendingSnapshot unique constraint (stateCode, fiscalYear, agencyId, scope) includes nullable agencyId; SQLite treats NULLs as distinct in UNIQUE constraints, allowing duplicate rows with NULL agencyId
resolutions.resolution: Use the standard @Unique decorator as-is. Accept that SQLite allows multiple NULL agencyId rows with the same (stateCode, fiscalYear, scope). Deduplication is enforced at application level in Epic 2's sync upsert logic.
resolutions.reason: TypeORM does not natively support partial unique indexes. Using a sentinel value would change the data model's semantic contract. Application-level enforcement is standard practice for nullable unique columns in SQLite.

resolutions.contradiction: perCapita fields (GeoSpendingSnapshot, DisasterFundingRecord) are monetary-derived; unclear if stored as cents/person integer or dollars/person float
resolutions.resolution: Store perCapita as integer (cents per person), consistent with the 'all money values are integers' rule. Frontend divides by 100 for display.
resolutions.reason: perCapita = obligatedAmount(cents) / population. Storing as integer cents/person avoids floating-point issues and maintains consistency with the all-money-is-cents convention.

resolutions.contradiction: recoveryRatio (DisasterRecoveryRatio) is a dimensionless ratio (fedSpending/fema), not a monetary amount — should it be integer or float?
resolutions.resolution: Store recoveryRatio as float/decimal type. It is a ratio, not a monetary value. The 'money as cents integer' rule does not apply.
resolutions.reason: recoveryRatio = fedSpendingObligated / femaObligated produces values like 2.5. Forcing to integer would lose precision on a non-monetary derived field.

resolutions.contradiction: File tree shows empty dto/ directories but git does not track empty directories
resolutions.resolution: Create each dto/ directory with a .gitkeep file so git tracks the directory. These are placeholders for Epic 3 DTOs.
resolutions.reason: File tree explicitly shows these directories. Creating them now prevents 'directory not found' issues when Epic 3 adds DTO files.

resolutions.contradiction: Full TDD mandate vs scaffolding stories (S01 install deps, S02 tsconfig) that have no behavior to test
resolutions.resolution: Every story still ships a spec. For scaffolding stories, the spec asserts structural invariants: AppModule compiles and is decorated (S01), @shared/interfaces resolves at runtime (S02). The spec shape is table-driven where there is branching logic; for structural assertions a flat testTable with one row per invariant is acceptable.
resolutions.reason: Keeping a spec in every story is non-negotiable per AGENTS.md Skill 2. Structural specs catch scaffold drift (missing deps, broken aliases) that would otherwise surface mid-build.

resolutions.contradiction: E4-S01 scaffolded @angular/material@^22 + @angular/cdk@^22 against @angular/core@^20 — Material v22 requires Angular v22 APIs, breaking the build. Also @angular/animations was missing (needed by provideAnimationsAsync).
resolutions.resolution: Material/CDK must match Angular core major version. Use @angular/material@^20.0.0 + @angular/cdk@^20.0.0 (latest 20.x LTS = 20.2.14). @angular/animations@^20.3.0 is a required dependency (provideAnimationsAsync lazy-loads it).
resolutions.reason: Material/CDK lag behind core in minor versions — 20.3.x does not exist for Material. Scope-leakage in E4-S04 (builder fixed package.json in worktree but it was not in scope.files) shipped a broken build to main. Caught by QA.
